<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<content id="x4_ai_influence" name="AI Influence" description="LLM-driven faction representatives you can talk to. Conversations route through the Neural Link bridge to a local LLM; validated actions feed back into the live X4 world." author="Moshine" version="100" date="2026-06-24" save="0" enabled="1">
  <text language="44" name="AI Influence" description="LLM-driven faction repres

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_chat" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <cues>
    <!-- Per-playthrough identity. Generates a random save_uuid ONCE and persists it in the save.
         Uses event_game_loaded (a VALID event condition — a bare non-event <check_value> as a root
         cue's only condition is illegal in X4: "event condition required"). Fires on every load;
         the do_if guard generates the uuid only when unset, so it persists across the playthrough.
         The bridge keys ALL memory/conversation by save_id, so a new game (new uuid) starts fresh. -->
    <cue name="Save_identity">
      <conditions>
        <check_any>
          <event_game_started />
          <event_game_loaded />
        </check_any>
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <do_if value="not Save_identity.$save_uuid?">
          <set_value name="$save_uuid" min="100000000" max="999999999" />
        </do_if>
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Library: open the comm-link window with a faction/target context + the per-save id.
         purpose="run_actions" — invoked via <run_actions ref=...>, not signalled. -->
    <library name="Open_chat" purpose="run_actions">
      <params>
        <param name="faction" default="'argon'" />
        <param name="target" default="'Faction Officer'" />
        <!-- Optional opener: an ME-wheel suggestion's fuller line. When set, the window opens and
             auto-sends it as the player's first message. Empty = free-text (player types). -->
        <param name="initial" default="''" />
      </params>
      <actions>
        <!-- Stable per-save id string sent to the bridge as save_id; default until identity gens. -->
        <set_value name="$sid" exact="'game_unset'" />
        <do_if value="md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid?">
          <set_value name="$sid" exact="'game_' + md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid" />
        </do_if>
        <!-- NPC stats from the conversation script's State (set at conversation start). Defaults when
             opened outside a conversation (e.g. the hotkey). -->
        <set_value name="$nskill" exact="if md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$skill? then md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$skill else 0" />
        <set_value name="$nrole" exact="if md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$role? then md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$role else 'crew'" />
        <!-- initial goes LAST; the Lua parser splits the param string on '|' then '='. -->
        <raise_lua_event name="'AIChat.open'"
          param="'faction_id=' + $faction + '|target_name=' + $target + '|save_id=' + $sid + '|npc_role=' + $nrole + '|npc_skill=' + $nskill + '|initial=' + $initial" />
      </actions>
    </library>

    <!-- Boot: start the 1s poll heartbeat. CONDITIONLESS on purpose — a cue with no <conditions> is
         "instantly true" and runs when the MD script (re)loads (game load AND `refreshmd`). NO
         instantiate (X4 rejects instantiate on a cue with no event/check interval). The auto-open
         test scaffold was REMOVED: the window opens ONLY from the walk-up "Speak to AI" choice,
         never on load/reload. -->
    <cue name="Chat_boot">
      <actions>
        <set_value name="$booted" exact="1" />
      </actions>
      <cues>
        <!-- Deterministic poll heartbeat: raise AIChat.poll every 1s, forever. -->
        <cue name="Poll_tick">
          <delay exact="1s" />
          <actions>
            <raise_lua_event name="'AIChat.poll'" />
            <reset_cue cue="Poll_tick" />
          </actions>
        </cue>
      </cues>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_contract" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <cues>
    <!-- Raise the Lua event that fires the djfhe HTTP round-trip to the Neural Link bridge.
         purpose="run_actions" so it's invoked via <run_actions ref=...> (libraries are NOT
         signalled — signal_cue is for cues with event_cue_signalled conditions). -->
    <library name="Request_action" purpose="run_actions">
      <actions>
        <raise_lua_event name="'ai_influence.request'" param="table[$faction='split']" />
      </actions>
    </library>
    <!-- Dispatcher: receive the validated action back from Lua and execute it with NATIVE X4
         verbs. The LLM only proposes a whitelisted label; THIS deterministic handler disposes
         (mirrors the bridge's Stage-3 validator in-game). Native verbs only — no DeadAir calls.
         Verbs/thresholds from docs/x4_action_cheatsheet.md (DeadAir as reference, not dependency). -->
    <cue name="On_action">
      <conditions>
        <event_ui_triggered screen="'ai_influence'" control="'action'" />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <set_value name="$act" exact="event.param3" />
        <set_value name="$type" exact="if $act.type? then $act.type else 'none'" />

        <!-- (1) Notification — immersion / safe fallback. -->
        <do_if value="$type == 'show_notification'">
          <show_notification text="if $act.message? then $act.message else 'AI Influence'" />
        </do_if>

        <!-- (2) Relation move = war/relation. adjust_relation adds a delta; set_relation is absolute.
             War/peace are NOT special verbs: you cross a relation threshold and the engine + faction
             AI take over. Fire the news only on the CROSSING so we never re-declare.
             NOTE: in X4 MD, <do_if>/<do_elseif>/<do_else> are CLOSED SIBLINGS, not nested. -->
        <do_elseif value="$type == 'adjust_relation' or $type == 'set_relation'">
          <set_value name="$WAR_ELIGIBLE" exact="-0.10" />
          <set_value name="$PEACE_ELIGIBLE" exact="-0.01" />
          <set_value name="$fidA" exact="if $act.faction? then $act.faction else ''" />
          <set_value name="$fidB" exact="if $act.target? then $act.target else 'player'" />
          <set_value name="$facA" exact="if faction.{$fidA}? then faction.{$fidA} else null" />
          <set_value name="$facB" exact="if faction.{$fidB}? then faction.{$fidB} else faction.player" />
          <do_if value="$facA and $facB and $act.relation?">
            <set_value name="$oldRel" exact="$facA.relationto.{$facB}" />
            <set_value name="$newRel" exact="if $type == 'set_relation' then $act.relation else $oldRel + $act.relation" />
            <do_if value="$newRel gt 1.0"><set_value name="$newRel" exact="1.0" /></do_if>
            <do_elseif value="$newRel lt (-1.0)"><set_value name="$newRel" exact="-1.0" /></do_elseif>
            <set_faction_relation faction="$facA" otherfaction="$facB" value="$newRel" />
            <!-- World-model write-back: report the relation we just changed so the bridge DB mirrors
                 reality + the dashboard's Influence Log shows it. (subject/object are the canon ids.) -->
            <set_value name="$rsid" exact="'game_unset'" />
            <do_if value="md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid?">
              <set_value name="$rsid" exact="'game_' + md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid" />
            </do_if>
            <raise_lua_event name="'AIChat.relation_report'"
              param="'subject=' + $fidA + '|object=' + $fidB + '|relation=' + $newRel + '|save_id=' + $rsid" />
            <do_if value="$oldRel gt $WAR_ELIGIBLE and $newRel le $WAR_ELIGIBLE">
              <write_to_logbook category="alerts"
                title="'WAR: ' + $facA.knownname + ' vs ' + $facB.knownname"
                text="'Hostilities have begun.'" faction="$facA" />
              <show_notification text="'War: ' + $facA.knownname + ' vs ' + $facB.knownname" />
            </do_if>
            <do_elseif value="$oldRel le $WAR_ELIGIBLE and $newRel gt $PEACE_ELIGIBLE">
              <write_to_logbook category="alerts"
                title="'PEACE: ' + $facA.knownname + ' and ' + $facB.knownname"
                text="'A ceasefire has taken hold.'" faction="$facA" />
              <show_notification text="'Peace: ' + $facA.knownname + ' and ' + $facB.knownname" />
            </do_elseif>
          </do_if>
        </do_elseif>

        <!-- (3) News — logbook-only immersion entry. -->
        <do_elseif value="$type == 'write_to_logbook' or $type == 'news'">
          <write_to_logbook category="general"
            title="if $act.title? then $act.title else 'Galactic News'"
            text="if $act.message? then $act.message else ''" />
        </do_elseif>

        <do_else>
          <debug_text text="'[AIINF-TEST] unhandled action: ' + $type" filter="general" />
        </do_else>
      </actions>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_conversation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <!-- Walk-up "Speak to AI" -> a Mass-Effect-style opener wheel: 3 LLM-generated short paraphrase
       options + "Type my own message". Picking a paraphrase opens the comm-link chat and auto-sends
       its fuller line; "Type my own" opens free text.

       Latency masking: the ~4s /api/suggest call is FIRED on conversation_started (pre-fetch), while
       the player is still navigating the Talk menu, so options are usually ready by the time they
       open the wheel. If not ready yet, the wheel shows "Type my own" + a Refresh option.

       Flow (native add_player_choice_sub, grounded on ExtendedConversationMenu_TestMod):
         conversation start -> stash npc + faction/name, raise AIChat.suggest, offer "Speak to AI"
         Lua GET /api/suggest -> AddUITriggeredEvent(control='suggestions') -> On_suggestions stores
         pick "Speak to AI" -> Speak_menu builds the wheel from stored options
         pick option N -> Open_chat(initial = line N) ; pick "Type my own" -> Open_chat (free text)
       Shift+C hotkey remains the fallback (conversation hooks are patch-fragile — see vault note). -->
  <cues>
    <!-- Static holder across the start -> select handoff (one conversation active at a time). -->
    <cue name="State" />

    <!-- Conversation start: stash partner + display strings, pre-fetch suggestions, offer the entry. -->
    <cue name="Add_speak_choice" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions>
        <check_any>
          <event_conversation_started conversation="default" />
          <event_conversation_returned_to_section section="default" />
        </check_any>
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$current_npc" exact="event.object" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$facname" exact="if event.object.owner? then event.object.owner.knownname else 'argon'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$npcname" exact="if event.object.knownname? then event.object.knownname else 'Officer'" />
        <!-- NPC stats (grounded vs vanilla md/Boarding.xml): overall skill + role. So the persona
             knows she's e.g. a seasoned marine, not a generic voice. -->
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$skill" exact="if event.object.combinedskill? then event.object.combinedskill else 0" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$role" exact="'crew'" />
        <do_if value="event.object.role? and event.object.role == entityrole.marine">
          <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$role" exact="'marine'" />
        </do_if>
        <do_elseif value="event.object.role? and event.object.role == entityrole.service">
          <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$role" exact="'service crew'" />
        </do_elseif>
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$ready" exact="false" />
        <!-- Per-save id (mirrors Open_chat) so suggestions are grounded in THIS playthrough. -->
        <set_value name="$sid" exact="'game_unset'" />
        <do_if value="md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid?">
          <set_value name="$sid" exact="'game_' + md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid" />
        </do_if>
        <raise_lua_event name="'AIChat.suggest'"
          param="'faction_id=' + md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$facname + '|target_name=' + md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$npcname + '|save_id=' + $sid" />
        <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_Speak" text="'Speak to AI'" />
        <!-- PROVING TEST (reliable post-refreshmd, unlike the Shift+W hotkey which misses the
             Hotkey_API.Reloaded window): declare war from THIS npc's faction onto the player. -->
        <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_ForceWar" text="'[TEST] Declare war on me'" />
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Proving slice: force THIS faction to war (-1.0) with the player and log the crossing.
         Deterministic (no LLM) — tests ONLY whether set_faction_relation produces real combat. -->
    <cue name="ForceWar_handler" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions>
        <event_conversation_next_section section="AIInf_ForceWar" />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <set_value name="$npc" exact="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$current_npc" />
        <do_if value="$npc and $npc.owner?">
          <set_value name="$A" exact="$npc.owner" />
          <set_value name="$old" exact="$A.relationto.{faction.player}" />
          <set_faction_relation faction="$A" otherfaction="faction.player" value="-1.0" />
          <write_to_logbook category="alerts"
            title="'[AI TEST] WAR: ' + $A.knownname + ' vs you'"
            text="'Forced relation to -1.0 (was ' + $old + '). Watch for ships engaging.'" faction="$A" />
          <show_notification text="'[AI TEST] ' + $A.knownname + ' now hostile to you (was ' + $old + ')'" />
        </do_if>
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Lua hands back the suggestions via AddUITriggeredEvent (event.param3 = {n, l1..3, t1..3}). -->
    <cue name="On_suggestions" instantiate="true">
      <conditions>
        <event_ui_triggered screen="'ai_influence'" control="'suggestions'" />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <set_value name="$d" exact="event.param3" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$l1" exact="if $d.$l1? then $d.$l1 else 'Ask a question'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$l2" exact="if $d.$l2? then $d.$l2 else 'Make a request'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$l3" exact="if $d.$l3? then $d.$l3 else 'Share news'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$t1" exact="if $d.$t1? then $d.$t1 else 'I have a question for you.'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$t2" exact="if $d.$t2? then $d.$t2 else 'I have a request.'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$t3" exact="if $d.$t3? then $d.$t3 else 'Have you heard the latest?'" />
        <set_value name="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$ready" exact="$d.$n? and ($d.$n ge 1)" />
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- "Speak to AI" picked: build the wheel. Suggestions only when ready (else Refresh placeholder). -->
    <cue name="Speak_menu" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions>
        <check_any>
          <event_conversation_next_section section="AIInf_Speak" />
          <event_conversation_returned_to_section section="AIInf_Speak" />
        </check_any>
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <do_if value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$ready">
          <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_p1" text="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$l1" />
          <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_p2" text="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$l2" />
          <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_p3" text="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$l3" />
        </do_if>
        <do_else>
          <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_Speak" text="'(thinking — pick to refresh suggestions)'" />
        </do_else>
        <add_player_choice_sub section="AIInf_typed" text="'Type my own message'" />
        <add_player_choice_return text="'Back'" />
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Pick handlers: open the chat. The three suggestion picks auto-send their fuller line. -->
    <cue name="Pick_typed" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions><event_conversation_next_section section="AIInf_typed" /></conditions>
      <actions>
        <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_chat.Open_chat">
          <param name="faction" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$facname" />
          <param name="target" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$npcname" />
        </run_actions>
      </actions>
    </cue>
    <cue name="Pick_1" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions><event_conversation_next_section section="AIInf_p1" /></conditions>
      <actions>
        <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_chat.Open_chat">
          <param name="faction" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$facname" />
          <param name="target" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$npcname" />
          <param name="initial" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$t1" />
        </run_actions>
      </actions>
    </cue>
    <cue name="Pick_2" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions><event_conversation_next_section section="AIInf_p2" /></conditions>
      <actions>
        <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_chat.Open_chat">
          <param name="faction" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$facname" />
          <param name="target" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$npcname" />
          <param name="initial" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$t2" />
        </run_actions>
      </actions>
    </cue>
    <cue name="Pick_3" instantiate="true" namespace="this">
      <conditions><event_conversation_next_section section="AIInf_p3" /></conditions>
      <actions>
        <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_chat.Open_chat">
          <param name="faction" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$facname" />
          <param name="target" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$npcname" />
          <param name="initial" value="md.ai_influence_conversation.State.$t3" />
        </run_actions>
      </actions>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_hotkey" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <!-- Keybind to open the AI Influence chat on demand, via SirNukes Hotkey API (sn_mod_support_apis).
       Registers a named action that appears in the hotkey options menu for rebinding, AND a default
       key (Shift+C) so it works out of the box. Requires sn_mod_support_apis installed. -->
  <cues>
    <cue name="Register_Hotkey" instantiate="true">
      <conditions>
        <event_cue_signalled cue="md.Hotkey_API.Reloaded" />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <signal_cue_instantly cue="md.Hotkey_API.Register_Action"
          param="table[
            $id          = 'ai_influence_open_chat',
            $name        = 'AI Influence: Open Chat',
            $description = 'Open the AI Influence comm-link chat window.',
            $category    = 'AI Influence',
            $onPress     = On_Hotkey,
            $contexts    = ['flying', 'walking']
          ]" />
        <!-- Default binding so it works immediately; rebind in the hotkey menu if it clashes. -->
        <signal_cue_instantly cue="md.Hotkey_API.Register_Key"
          param="table[$key = 'shift c', $id = 'ai_influence_open_chat']" />
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Key pressed → open the chat window. -->
    <cue name="On_Hotkey" instantiate="true">
      <conditions>
        <event_cue_signalled />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_chat.Open_chat">
          <param name="faction" value="'argon'" />
          <param name="target" value="'Argon Officer'" />
        </run_actions>
      </actions>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_main" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <cues>
    <cue name="Start">
      <conditions>
        <event_game_loaded />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <!-- Load confirmation only. The legacy <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_contract.
             Request_action"> ping was removed: it resolved to null on event_game_loaded (cross-
             script library load-order) and spammed 2 errors. The real round-trip now flows through
             the chat window -> djfhe -> bridge -> On_action dispatcher, not this cue. -->
        <show_notification text="'AI Influence Test Mod loaded.'" />
      </actions>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_proving" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <!-- INFLUENCE PROVING SLICE — step 0: validate the make-or-break unknown.
       Does crossing a faction-relation threshold with the native verb set_faction_relation actually
       produce HOSTILITY in-game (ships open fire), or just change a number? Everything downstream (the
       influence engine) is wasted effort if this is a no — so prove it FIRST with a deterministic
       forced trigger, isolated from the LLM.

       A SirNukes Hotkey (default Shift+W) forces a chosen faction pair to war (-1.0) and logs the
       before value. RUN ON A THROWAWAY SAVE. Default flips Teladi hostile to the PLAYER (observable
       anywhere you meet Teladi ships). For the faction-vs-faction thesis, change $B below to another
       faction (e.g. faction.argon) and fly to where both operate.
       Requires sn_mod_support_apis (already an optional dependency of this mod). -->
  <cues>
    <cue name="Register_Test_Hotkey" instantiate="true">
      <conditions>
        <event_cue_signalled cue="md.Hotkey_API.Reloaded" />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <signal_cue_instantly cue="md.Hotkey_API.Register_Action"
          param="table[
            $id          = 'ai_influence_force_war',
            $name        = '[AI TEST] Force faction war',
            $description = 'Proving test: set a faction pair to war (-1.0) and watch for combat. Use a throwaway save.',
            $category    = 'AI Influence',
            $onPress     = On_Force_War,
            $contexts    = ['flying', 'walking']
          ]" />
        <signal_cue_instantly cue="md.Hotkey_API.Register_Key"
          param="table[$key = 'shift w', $id = 'ai_influence_force_war']" />
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Key pressed → force the relation to war and log the crossing. Mirrors the contract's
         set_relation dispatch path, but deterministic (no LLM) so this tests ONLY the game mechanic. -->
    <cue name="On_Force_War" instantiate="true">
      <conditions>
        <event_cue_signalled />
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <!-- EDIT THESE to test other pairs. Set to Alliance of the Word (the NPCs near the player). -->
        <set_value name="$A" exact="faction.alliance" />
        <set_value name="$B" exact="faction.player" />
        <do_if value="$A and $B">
          <set_value name="$old" exact="$A.relationto.{$B}" />
          <set_faction_relation faction="$A" otherfaction="$B" value="-1.0" />
          <write_to_logbook category="alerts"
            title="'[AI TEST] WAR: ' + $A.knownname + ' vs ' + $B.knownname"
            text="'Forced relation to -1.0 (was ' + $old + '). Watch for ships engaging.'" faction="$A" />
          <show_notification text="'[AI TEST] ' + $A.knownname + ' now hostile to ' + $B.knownname + ' (was ' + $old + ')'" />
        </do_if>
      </actions>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<mdscript name="ai_influence_worldsync" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="md.xsd">
  <!-- WORLD-MODEL SYNC: keep the bridge DB mirroring the live game's faction relations.
       - ON LOAD/NEW GAME: read all relations immediately (fixes save-load desync + id fragmentation).
       - PERIODICALLY (every 60s): re-read, so X4's OWN faction AI changes, the player's rep gains/
         losses, and any non-dispatch change also reach the DB — not just our explicit dispatches.
       Both call the same Do_sync library. Grounded: enumerates KNOWN faction ids and reads relations
       with the exact properties the contract uses (faction.{id} + .relationto.{factionobj}). -->
  <cues>
    <!-- The enumeration, shared by the on-load and periodic triggers. -->
    <library name="Do_sync" purpose="run_actions">
      <actions>
        <set_value name="$sid" exact="'game_unset'" />
        <do_if value="md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid?">
          <set_value name="$sid" exact="'game_' + md.ai_influence_chat.Save_identity.$save_uuid" />
        </do_if>
        <set_value name="$ids" exact="['argon', 'antigone', 'alliance', 'teladi', 'ministry', 'paranid', 'holyorder', 'split', 'freesplit', 'scaleplate', 'xenon', 'khaak', 'player']" />
        <set_value name="$report" exact="''" />
        <do_for_each name="$idA" in="$ids">
          <do_for_each name="$idB" in="$ids">
            <do_if value="$idA != $idB and faction.{$idA}? and faction.{$idB}?">
              <set_value name="$rel" exact="faction.{$idA}.relationto.{faction.{$idB}}" />
              <set_value name="$report" exact="$report + $idA + '~' + $idB + '~' + $rel + ';'" />
            </do_if>
          </do_for_each>
        </do_for_each>
        <raise_lua_event name="'AIChat.sync_relations'" param="'save_id=' + $sid + '||' + $report" />
      </actions>
    </library>

    <!-- On a new game OR a save load: sync immediately. -->
    <cue name="Sync_on_load" instantiate="true">
      <conditions>
        <check_any>
          <event_game_started />
          <event_game_loaded />
        </check_any>
      </conditions>
      <actions>
        <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_worldsync.Do_sync" />
      </actions>
    </cue>

    <!-- Periodic re-sync every 60s (conditionless parent runs at load; Tick loops). Catches X4's own
         faction-AI changes + the player's rep changes between loads. -->
    <cue name="Sync_periodic">
      <actions>
        <set_value name="$started" exact="1" />
      </actions>
      <cues>
        <cue name="Tick">
          <delay exact="15s" />
          <actions>
            <run_actions ref="md.ai_influence_worldsync.Do_sync" />
            <reset_cue cue="Tick" />
          </actions>
        </cue>
      </cues>
    </cue>
  </cues>
</mdscript>

-- AI Influence Test Mod — Lua side of the round-trip.
-- MD raises "ai_influence.request"; we POST/GET the Neural Link bridge via djfhe,
-- then raise an event_ui_triggered back to MD carrying the validated action.
-- djfhe is required LAZILY inside the handler — our UI Lua can load before djfhe's init.lua,
-- so a top-level require would fail "module not found". By event time djfhe is loaded.
local function on_request(_, payload)
  local okr, Request = pcall(require, "djfhe.http.request")
  local okj, json = pcall(require, "jsonlua.json")
  if not okr or not Request then
    AddUITriggeredEvent("ai_influence", "action", { type = "error", message = "djfhe_http not loaded" })
    return
  end
  local faction = (payload and payload.faction) or "split"
  -- djfhe fluent API: Request.new(method):setUrl():send(callback). (NOT Request:new({...}).)
  Request.new("GET")
    :setUrl("http://127.0.0.1:8713/api/test/llm_action?faction=" .. tostring(faction))
    :send(function(response, err)
      if err then
        AddUITriggeredEvent("ai_influence", "action", { type = "error", message = tostring(err) })
        return
      end
      local body = response and response:getBody() or "{}"
      local ok, decoded = pcall(json.decode, body)
      local msg = "Neural Link test: no message"
      if ok and decoded and decoded.action and decoded.action.params and decoded.action.params.message then
        msg = decoded.action.params.message
      end
      AddUITriggeredEvent("ai_influence", "action", { type = "show_notification", message = msg })
    end)
end

RegisterEvent("ai_influence.request", on_request)

---@diagnostic disable: undefined-global, need-check-nil, undefined-field
-- ============================================================================
-- AI INFLUENCE CHAT - HTTP Bridge Client (djfhe_http)
-- POST /v1/request  -> submit a player chat turn
-- GET  /v1/updates_pool -> drain completed replies, write to logbook
-- Poll is driven deterministically by an MD 1s loop raising "AIChat.poll".
-- ============================================================================

local LOAD_REVISION = "aichat-r1"
local BRIDGE_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:8713"

local function log(msg)
    if DebugError then DebugError("[AICHAT][UIX] " .. tostring(msg)) end
end
log("STARTING LOAD rev=" .. LOAD_REVISION)

-- Read Helper lazily — it is nil at file load (see X4_STANDALONE_MENU_SCHEMA / aic_menu.lua).
-- Caching the load-time value would leave Helper nil forever.
local Helper = rawget(_G, "Helper")
local function refreshHelper() if not Helper then Helper = rawget(_G, "Helper") end return Helper end

local ffi = require("ffi")
local C = ffi.C
ffi.cdef [[
    void AddPlayerLogEntry(const char* category, const char* title, const char* text);
    int64_t GetCurrentUTCDataTime(void);
]]

-- ---- djfhe http (CONSUMER API ONLY) ----------------------------------------
-- Require ONLY the request module and use the fluent API:
--   Request.new(METHOD):setUrl():setBody():send(callback)
-- The :send() enqueues into djfhe's own internal client, advanced by djfhe's MD poll.
-- Do NOT require "djfhe.http.client" (it's djfhe-internal) and do NOT add a broad
-- "extensions/?.lua" to package.path — either one poisons djfhe's module cache and
-- breaks its update loop forever ("loop or previous error loading module 'djfhe.http.client'").
-- djfhe registers its OWN package.path in its init.lua, so we just require its public
-- module — but LAZILY (on first use), NOT at file load. Our UI Lua can load before djfhe's
-- init.lua runs; by the time the player sends a chat, djfhe is loaded and the require resolves.
local Request, json = nil, nil
local function ensureDjfhe()
    if Request then return true end
    local ok_r, req = pcall(require, "djfhe.http.request")
    if ok_r and req then Request = req end
    if not json then local ok_j, js = pcall(require, "jsonlua.json"); if ok_j then json = js end end
    if Request then log("djfhe request module loaded (lazy).") end
    return Request ~= nil
end

local function newRequest(method)
    if not ensureDjfhe() then return nil end
    if Request and Request.new then return Request.new(method) end
    if type(Request) == "function" then return Request(method) end
    return nil
end

local function writeToLogbook(title, text)
    refreshHelper()
    pcall(function() C.AddPlayerLogEntry("news", title, text) end)
    if Helper and Helper.showNotification then
        Helper.showNotification({ title = title, text = text, icon = "npc_contact_01", priority = 1 })
    end
end

-- ---- public API ------------------------------------------------------------
if not AI_Influence then AI_Influence = {} end
AI_Influence.processedRequestIds = {}
AI_Influence._autoOpened = false

function AI_Influence.SendToBridge(payload, callback)
    local req = newRequest("POST")
    if not req then
        if callback then callback(false, nil, "djfhe request module missing") end
        return
    end
    req:setUrl(BRIDGE_URL .. "/v1/request")
    -- Keep the known-working body format (JSON string); djfhe sends it as-is.
    req:setBody((json and json.encode) and json.encode(payload) or payload)
    log("POST /v1/request request_id=" .. tostring(payload and payload.request_id))
    req:send(function(resp, err)
        if err then
            if callback then callback(false, nil, "HTTP Error: " .. tostring(err)) end
            return
        end
        local status = (resp and resp.getStatus) and resp:getStatus() or 0
        -- /v1/request returns 202 Accepted (not 200) — accept both.
        if status ~= 200 and status ~= 202 then
            if callback then callback(false, nil, "HTTP Status " .. tostring(status)) end
            return
        end
        local content, jerr = resp:getJson()
        if jerr or not (content and content.ok) then
            if callback then callback(false, nil, "Bridge rejected request") end
            return
        end
        if callback then callback(true, { request_id = content.request_id }, nil) end
    end)
end

function AI_Influence.PollUpdates(callback)
    local req = newRequest("GET")
    if not req then return end
    req:setUrl(BRIDGE_URL .. "/v1/updates_pool")
    req:send(function(resp, err)
        if err then return end
        local status = (resp and resp.getStatus) and resp:getStatus() or 0
        if status ~= 200 then return end
        local content, jerr = resp:getJson()
        if not jerr and content and content.updates and #content.updates > 0 then
            if callback then callback(true, content.updates, nil) end
        end
    end)
end

-- ---- NPC registry: index encounterable/named NPCs + the player into the bridge -------------
-- Param string (from MD): "save_id=...|player=...|npcs=Name~faction;Name~faction;". Posted to
-- /v1/npcs/index, which upserts identity WITHOUT clobbering any existing Player2 binding.
function AI_Influence.IndexNpcs(param)
    local req = newRequest("POST")
    if not req then return end
    local ctx = {}
    for pair in string.gmatch(tostring(param or ""), "([^|]+)") do
        local eq = string.find(pair, "=")
        if eq then ctx[string.sub(pair, 1, eq - 1)] = string.sub(pair, eq + 1) end
    end
    local npcs = {}
    for entry in string.gmatch(ctx.npcs or "", "([^;]+)") do
        local sep = string.find(entry, "~")
        if sep then
            npcs[#npcs + 1] = { name = string.sub(entry, 1, sep - 1), faction_id = string.sub(entry, sep + 1) }
        elseif entry ~= "" then
            npcs[#npcs + 1] = { name = entry }
        end
    end
    local body = { save_id = ctx.save_id or "unindexed", player = { name = ctx.player or "Player" }, npcs = npcs }
    req:setUrl(BRIDGE_URL .. "/v1/npcs/index")
    req:setBody((json and json.encode) and json.encode(body) or body)
    log("index_npcs POST count=" .. tostring(#npcs) .. " save=" .. tostring(ctx.save_id))
    req:send(function(resp, err) if err then log("index_npcs err: " .. tostring(err)) end end)
end

local function onIndexNpcs(_, param) AI_Influence.IndexNpcs(param) end

-- ---- ME-wheel suggestions: ask the bridge for short contextual openers, hand them to MD ------
-- MD raises "AIChat.suggest" with "faction_id=..|target_name=..|save_id=.." on conversation start
-- (pre-fetch). We GET /api/suggest and, when it returns ~4s later, push the {label,line} list back
-- to MD via AddUITriggeredEvent so the conversation wheel can build its choices.
function AI_Influence.RequestSuggestions(param)
    local req = newRequest("GET")
    if not req then return end
    local ctx = {}
    for pair in string.gmatch(tostring(param or ""), "([^|]+)") do
        local eq = string.find(pair, "=")
        if eq then ctx[string.sub(pair, 1, eq - 1)] = string.sub(pair, eq + 1) end
    end
    local function enc(s) return (string.gsub(tostring(s or ""), "[^%w%-_%.]", function(c)
        return string.format("%%%02X", string.byte(c)) end)) end
    req:setUrl(BRIDGE_URL .. "/api/suggest?save_id=" .. enc(ctx.save_id)
        .. "&faction_id=" .. enc(ctx.faction_id) .. "&npc_name=" .. enc(ctx.target_name))
    log("suggest GET npc=" .. tostring(ctx.target_name))
    req:send(function(resp, err)
        if err then return end
        local status = (resp and resp.getStatus) and resp:getStatus() or 0
        if status ~= 200 then return end
        local content = resp:getJson()
        local s = content and content.suggestions
        if type(s) ~= "table" then return end
        local out = { n = #s }
        for i = 1, #s do out["l" .. i] = s[i].label or ""; out["t" .. i] = s[i].line or "" end
        log("suggest <= " .. tostring(#s) .. " options")
        AddUITriggeredEvent("ai_influence", "suggestions", out)
    end)
end

local function onRequestSuggest(_, param) AI_Influence.RequestSuggestions(param) end

-- ---- world-model write-back: report a relation the dispatcher just changed in-game --------------
-- MD On_action raises "AIChat.relation_report" with "subject=..|object=..|relation=..|save_id=..".
function AI_Influence.ReportRelation(param)
    local req = newRequest("POST")
    if not req then return end
    local ctx = {}
    for pair in string.gmatch(tostring(param or ""), "([^|]+)") do
        local eq = string.find(pair, "=")
        if eq then ctx[string.sub(pair, 1, eq - 1)] = string.sub(pair, eq + 1) end
    end
    local body = { save_id = ctx.save_id, subject = ctx.subject, object = ctx.object,
                   relation = tonumber(ctx.relation) or 0, source = "mod_dispatch" }
    req:setUrl(BRIDGE_URL .. "/v1/relation_report")
    req:setBody((json and json.encode) and json.encode(body) or body)
    log("relation_report POST " .. tostring(ctx.subject) .. "->" .. tostring(ctx.object) .. " = " .. tostring(ctx.relation))
    req:send(function(resp, err) if err then log("relation_report err: " .. tostring(err)) end end)
end

local function onReportRelation(_, param) AI_Influence.ReportRelation(param) end

-- ---- sync-on-load: push the game's ACTUAL faction relations so the DB mirrors reality -----------
-- MD raises "AIChat.sync_relations" with "save_id=<sid>||idA~idB~rel;idA~idB~rel;..." on game load.
function AI_Influence.SyncRelations(param)
    local req = newRequest("POST")
    if not req then return end
    local sid, body = string.match(tostring(param or ""), "save_id=([^|]*)||(.*)")
    sid = (sid ~= nil and sid ~= "") and sid or "unindexed"
    local rels = {}
    for entry in string.gmatch(body or "", "([^;]+)") do
        local a, b, r = string.match(entry, "([^~]+)~([^~]+)~(.+)")
        if a and b and r then rels[#rels + 1] = { subject = a, object = b, relation = tonumber(r) or 0 } end
    end
    req:setUrl(BRIDGE_URL .. "/v1/relations_sync")
    local payload = { save_id = sid, relations = rels }
    req:setBody((json and json.encode) and json.encode(payload) or payload)
    log("relations_sync POST count=" .. tostring(#rels) .. " save=" .. tostring(sid))
    req:send(function(resp, err) if err then log("relations_sync err: " .. tostring(err)) end end)
end

local function onSyncRelations(_, param) AI_Influence.SyncRelations(param) end

-- ---- single poll handler (drains replies, routes to logbook + actions) -----
local function handleUpdates(success, updates)
    if not (success and updates) then return end
    for _, update in ipairs(updates) do
        local text = update.text or update.reply or ""
        local author = update.author_name or update.author or update.name or "AI"

        -- NPC-state colour feedback on the open window, if any
        local menu = rawget(_G, "X4_Terminal_Menu")
        if update.state and menu and update.state ~= menu.npcState then
            menu.npcState = update.state
            if menu.active and menu.display then menu.display() end
        end

        if text ~= "" then
            log("logbook <= " .. tostring(author) .. ": " .. tostring(text))
            writeToLogbook(author, text)
            -- Append the NPC reply to the in-window transcript and refresh if the window is open.
            if menu then
                menu.history = menu.history or {}
                table.insert(menu.history, { role = "assistant", text = text })
                if menu.active and menu.display then menu.display() end
            end
        end

        -- actions (exactly once): gate confirm-required ones (influence/set_relation) behind a typed
        -- "yes" in the chat; dispatch the rest immediately. No silent war-declarations on the save.
        if update.actions and type(update.actions) == "table" and #update.actions > 0 then
            local reqId = update.request_id
            if reqId and not AI_Influence.processedRequestIds[reqId] then
                AI_Influence.processedRequestIds[reqId] = true
                for _, action in ipairs(update.actions) do
                    local actionType = action.type or action.action or "none"
                    local actionArgs = action.args or action.params or {}
                    local triggerParams = { type = actionType }
                    for k, v in pairs(actionArgs) do
                        if k == "relation" then triggerParams[k] = tonumber(v) or 0 else triggerParams[k] = v end
                    end
                    if action.needs_confirm and menu then
                        menu._pendingAction = triggerParams   -- aic_menu.onInput dispatches on "yes"
                        menu.history = menu.history or {}
                        table.insert(menu.history, { role = "assistant",
                            text = "[Proposal] " .. tostring(action.description or "An action is proposed.")
                                .. " Reply 'yes' to confirm, or anything else to decline." })
                        if menu.active and menu.display then menu.display() end
                        log("pending action held for confirm: " .. tostring(actionType))
                    else
                        log("UI action (immediate): " .. tostring(actionType))
                        AddUITriggeredEvent("ai_influence", "action", triggerParams)
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    end
end

-- Forward declaration so onPollTick (above onOpenCommLink) can fire the one-shot open.
local onOpenCommLink

local function onPollTick(_, _)
    -- Register the menu with the engine as soon as the global Helper exists (it is nil at file
    -- load — see aic_menu.lua). The MD 1s poll starts right after load, so the menu is registered
    -- a few seconds BEFORE Auto_open fires. This mirrors SirNukes' standalone_menu.lua, which
    -- defers its Init (table.insert(Menus)/Helper.registerMenu) until the menu env is ready via
    -- Register_Require_With_Init, rather than registering at file scope. (ref: bvbohnen/x4-projects
    -- sn_mod_support_apis ui/simple_menu/standalone_menu.lua)
    local m = rawget(_G, "X4_Terminal_Menu")
    if m and m.ensureRegistered and not m._registered then m.ensureRegistered() end
    -- (Removed the one-shot auto-open test scaffold: the window must only open from a real player
    -- action — the walk-up "Speak to AI" conversation choice — never on UI reload.)
    -- Auto-send a chosen ME-wheel opener once the window is actually open (set in onOpenCommLink).
    if m and m.active and m._pendingInitial and m.onInput then
        local t = m._pendingInitial; m._pendingInitial = nil
        log("auto-send opener: " .. tostring(t))
        pcall(m.onInput, t)
    end
    AI_Influence.PollUpdates(handleUpdates)
end

-- ---- open the comm window --------------------------------------------------
onOpenCommLink = function(_, params)
    log("onOpenCommLink")
    refreshHelper()
    local context = {}
    if type(params) == "string" then
        local sepStart, sepEnd = string.find(params, "||", 1, true)
        local contextStr = params
        if sepStart then contextStr = string.sub(params, sepEnd + 1) end
        for pair in string.gmatch(contextStr, "([^|]+)") do
            local eqPos = string.find(pair, "=")
            if eqPos then context[string.sub(pair, 1, eqPos - 1)] = string.sub(pair, eqPos + 1) end
        end
    end
    local termMenu = rawget(_G, "X4_Terminal_Menu")
    if not termMenu then
        local all = rawget(_G, "Menus")
        if all then for _, m in ipairs(all) do if m.name == "X4_Terminal" then termMenu = m break end end end
    end
    -- Definitive diagnostics: the window not appearing is almost always (a) the menu object
    -- never registered, or (b) display() erroring. Log both so the next reload's debuglog says
    -- EXACTLY where it dies instead of us guessing.
    log("onOpenCommLink: X4_Terminal_Menu " .. (termMenu and "FOUND" or "MISSING -> aic_menu.lua did not load/register"))
    if termMenu then
        termMenu.currentContext = {
            faction = context["faction_id"] or context["$faction_id"] or "argon",
            target  = context["target_name"] or context["$target_name"] or "Faction Officer",
            -- Per-save id from MD (Save_identity.$save_uuid). Keys bridge memory/chat to THIS
            -- playthrough so a new game starts fresh. nil if MD didn't send it (bridge falls back).
            save_id = context["save_id"] or context["$save_id"],
            full_context = context,
        }
        -- ME-wheel opener: if the player picked a suggested line, queue it to auto-send once the
        -- window is active (the poll tick does the send, so it waits for the engine to open it).
        local initial = context["initial"] or context["$initial"]
        termMenu._pendingInitial = (initial ~= nil and initial ~= "") and initial or nil
        if Helper and Helper.closeInteractMenu then pcall(Helper.closeInteractMenu) end
        -- Complete the engine registration NOW (open time), when the global Helper is populated —
        -- at file load it was nil, so registerMenu was skipped and OpenMenu had nothing to open.
        if termMenu.ensureRegistered then
            local reg = termMenu.ensureRegistered()
            log("ensureRegistered before open => engineRegistered=" .. tostring(reg))
        end
        -- GROUNDED FIX (ref: SirNukes simple_menu/Standalone_Menu.lua): a registered menu is opened
        -- by the ENGINE function OpenMenu(name,...), which then calls menu.onShowMenu() -> the frame
        -- build. We were calling onShowMenu/RaiseEvent directly, which builds a frame the engine
        -- never actually opens -> nothing renders. OpenMenu is the missing piece. currentContext is
        -- set above so onShowMenu reads it when the engine fires it (after a short delay).
        if OpenMenu then
            log("OpenMenu('" .. termMenu.name .. "') registeredInMenus=" .. tostring((function()
                local all = rawget(_G, "Menus"); if not all then return false end
                for _, m in ipairs(all) do if m.name == termMenu.name then return true end end; return false
            end)()))
            local ok, err = pcall(OpenMenu, termMenu.name, nil, nil, true)
            log("OpenMenu returned " .. (ok and "OK" or ("ERROR: " .. tostring(err))))
        elseif termMenu.onShowMenu then
            local ok, err = pcall(termMenu.onShowMenu)
            log("OpenMenu missing; onShowMenu fallback " .. (ok and "OK" or ("ERROR: " .. tostring(err))))
        end
    end
end

local function init()
    RegisterEvent("AIChat.open", onOpenCommLink)
    RegisterEvent("AIChat.poll", onPollTick)
    RegisterEvent("AIChat.index_npcs", onIndexNpcs)
    RegisterEvent("AIChat.suggest", onRequestSuggest)
    RegisterEvent("AIChat.relation_report", onReportRelation)
    RegisterEvent("AIChat.sync_relations", onSyncRelations)
    log("events registered: AIChat.open, AIChat.poll, AIChat.index_npcs, AIChat.suggest, AIChat.relation_report, AIChat.sync_relations")
end
init()

-- ============================================================================
-- AI INFLUENCE CHAT — menu shell GENERATED to the X4 Forge UIBuilder "standard" template
-- (the proven render pattern, verified in-game + against 24 vanilla menus): read Helper LAZILY
-- (nil at file load), register DEFERRED the moment Helper exists, then OpenMenu(name) → engine
-- calls onShowMenu → Helper.createFrameHandle → frame:display(). Caching Helper at load or
-- registering at file scope silently breaks rendering (X4_STANDALONE_MENU_SCHEMA).
-- Widgets (designer order): header, chat/transcript, input, SEND button, CLOSE button.
-- The djfhe/LLM transport is companion logic in aic_uix.lua (this file is the window).
-- ============================================================================

-- Helper is nil when this file first loads — read it lazily and re-fetch at use time.
local Helper = rawget(_G, "Helper")
local function refreshHelper() if not Helper then Helper = rawget(_G, "Helper") end return Helper end

local ffi = require("ffi")
local C = ffi.C
ffi.cdef [[
    void AddPlayerLogEntry(const char* category, const char* title, const char* text);
    int64_t GetCurrentUTCDataTime(void);
    const char* GetPlayerName(void);
]]

local function playerName()
    local ok, name = pcall(function() return ffi.string(C.GetPlayerName()) end)
    if ok and name and name ~= "" then return name end
    return "Player"
end

-- Menu table. Exposed as a global so the companion transport (aic_uix.lua) can find it.
X4_Terminal_Menu = {
    name = "X4_Terminal",
    layer = 4,
    active = false,
    currentContext = { faction = "argon", target = "Faction Officer" },
    editboxText = "",
    npcState = "normal",
    history = {},
}
local menu = X4_Terminal_Menu

local function log(m) if DebugError then DebugError("[AICHAT][MENU] " .. tostring(m)) end end

-- Idempotent + DEFERRED registration. Completes the engine registration (which OpenMenu needs) the
-- moment Helper is available. Called from the poll tick / open path, by which time Helper exists.
function menu.ensureRegistered()
    refreshHelper()
    _G.Menus = _G.Menus or {}
    local found = false
    for i, m in ipairs(_G.Menus) do if m.name == menu.name then _G.Menus[i] = menu; found = true; break end end
    if not found then table.insert(_G.Menus, menu) end
    if Helper and Helper.registerMenu and not menu._registered then
        local ok = pcall(Helper.registerMenu, menu); menu._registered = ok
    end
    log("ensureRegistered Helper=" .. tostring(Helper ~= nil) .. " registered=" .. tostring(menu._registered == true) .. " #Menus=" .. tostring(#_G.Menus))
    return menu._registered == true
end

-- PUBLIC: open the window. Pass a context table; onShowMenu reads it when the engine fires.
function menu.open(context)
    if type(context) == "table" then menu.currentContext = context end
    menu.ensureRegistered()
    if OpenMenu then log("OpenMenu"); OpenMenu(menu.name, nil, nil, true)
    elseif menu.onShowMenu then menu.onShowMenu() end
end

-- Engine entry: called by OpenMenu after a short delay.
function menu.onShowMenu()
    refreshHelper()
    menu.active = true
    menu.display()
end

function menu.showMenuCallback(_, context)
    if type(context) == "table" then menu.currentContext = context end
    menu.onShowMenu()
end

-- Render the frame + widget rows (UIBuilder standard-template body).
function menu.display()
    refreshHelper()
    log("display ENTER Helper=" .. tostring(Helper ~= nil) .. " Color=" .. tostring(rawget(_G, "Color") ~= nil))
    if not Helper then log("display ABORT: Helper nil"); return end
    if menu.frame then Helper.clearDataForRefresh(menu, menu.layer) end

    local w = Helper.scaleX(640)
    local h = Helper.scaleY(330)
    local x = ((Helper.viewWidth or 1920) - w) / 2
    -- Anchor near the BOTTOM of the screen (not dead-centre) so the NPC stays visible behind it.
    local y = (Helper.viewHeight or 1080) - h - Helper.scaleY(70)
    menu.frame = Helper.createFrameHandle(menu, { x = x, y = y, width = w, height = h, layer = menu.layer, standardButtons = { close = true } })

    local frameColor = Color["frame_background_semitransparent"]
    if menu.npcState == "scared" then frameColor = Color["row_background_warning"]
    elseif menu.npcState == "aggressive" then frameColor = Color["row_background_error"] end
    menu.frame:setBackground("solid", { color = frameColor })

    local ftable = menu.frame:addTable(2, { tabOrder = 1, width = w, highlightMode = "off" })
    ftable:setColWidthPercent(1, 78)
    ftable:setColWidthPercent(2, 22)
    local row

    -- header widget
    row = ftable:addRow(false, { bgColor = Color["row_title_background"] })
    row[1]:setColSpan(2):createText("Comm-Link: " .. (menu.currentContext.target or "NPC"), Helper.headerRowCenteredProperties)

    -- chat/transcript widget: fixed row count — newest at the BOTTOM, blank rows padded on top — so
    -- the input box stays anchored low instead of drifting as the conversation fills. (Single-table
    -- layout: the multi-table vanilla scroll approach needs explicit per-table y-offsets — TODO.)
    local hist = menu.history or {}
    local SLOTS = 7
    local startIdx = math.max(1, #hist - SLOTS + 1)
    local shown = {}
    for i = startIdx, #hist do shown[#shown + 1] = hist[i] end
    for p = 1, (SLOTS - #shown) do
        row = ftable:addRow(false, {})   -- transparent padding (no grey bar) to keep the input anchored
        local placeholder = (#shown == 0 and p == (SLOTS - #shown)) and "Say something to begin the conversation." or " "
        row[1]:setColSpan(2):createText(placeholder)
    end
    for _, item in ipairs(shown) do
        local who = (item.role == "user") and "You" or (menu.currentContext.target or "NPC")
        row = ftable:addRow(false, { bgColor = Color["row_background_unselectable"] })
        row[1]:setColSpan(2):createText(who .. ":  " .. tostring(item.text), { wordwrap = true })
    end

    -- input widget + SEND button
    row = ftable:addRow(true, { bgColor = Color["row_background_unselectable"] })
    row[1]:createEditBox({ height = Helper.standardButtonHeight, defaultText = "Enter message...", maxChars = 255, selectTextOnActivation = true })
    row[1].handlers.onTextChanged = function(_, text, textchanged) if textchanged and text ~= nil then menu.editboxText = text end end
    row[1].handlers.onEditBoxDeactivated = function(_, text, textchanged, isconfirmed)
        if textchanged and text ~= nil then menu.editboxText = text end
        if isconfirmed then menu.onInput(text) end
    end
    row[2]:createButton({ active = true }):setText("SEND", { halign = "center" })
    row[2].handlers.onClick = function() menu.onInput(menu.editboxText) end

    -- CLOSE button
    row = ftable:addRow(true, { bgColor = Color["row_background"] })
    row[1]:setColSpan(2):createButton({ active = true }):setText("CLOSE", { halign = "center" })
    row[1].handlers.onClick = function() menu.closeMenu() end

    menu.frame:display()
    log("display DONE frame=" .. tostring(menu.frame ~= nil))
end

-- Send a player line to the bridge (companion transport is the global AI_Influence in aic_uix.lua).
function menu.onInput(text)
    text = tostring(text or "")
    text = string.gsub(text, "^%s+", "")
    text = string.gsub(text, "%s+$", "")
    if text == "" then return end

    -- Confirm gate: if an influence action is pending (held by aic_uix.handleUpdates), a
    -- 'yes'/'confirm' DISPATCHES it; anything else declines and is sent as a normal chat turn.
    if menu._pendingAction then
        local pending = menu._pendingAction
        menu._pendingAction = nil
        local low = string.lower(text)
        if low == "yes" or low == "y" or low == "confirm" or low == "do it" then
            menu.history = menu.history or {}
            table.insert(menu.history, { role = "user", text = text })
            table.insert(menu.history, { role = "assistant", text = "[Confirmed] Dispatching." })
            menu.editboxText = ""
            if menu.active and menu.display then menu.display() end
            if AddUITriggeredEvent then AddUITriggeredEvent("ai_influence", "action", pending) end
            return
        else
            menu.history = menu.history or {}
            table.insert(menu.history, { role = "assistant", text = "[Declined the proposal.]" })
            -- fall through: send this message as a normal chat turn
        end
    end

    log("SEND: " .. text)
    menu.history = menu.history or {}
    table.insert(menu.history, { role = "user", text = text })
    menu.editboxText = ""
    if menu.active and menu.display then menu.display() end

    local requestId = "chat_" .. tostring(tonumber(C.GetCurrentUTCDataTime()))
    local bridge = rawget(_G, "AI_Influence")
    if not bridge or not bridge.SendToBridge then
        pcall(function() C.AddPlayerLogEntry("news", "AI Error", "Bridge client not loaded in the X4 UI runtime.") end)
        return
    end
    bridge.SendToBridge({
        request_id = requestId,
        faction_id = menu.currentContext.faction or "argon",
        npc_name = menu.currentContext.target,   -- the NPC's real personal name (e.g. "Selaia Erris")
        save_id = menu.currentContext.save_id,
        source = "pop-up",
        trigger_type = "player_chat",
        user_text = text,
        player_name = playerName(),
        prompt_vars = menu.currentContext.full_context or {},
    }, function(success, _, err)
        if success then log("bridge accepted " .. requestId)
        else pcall(function() C.AddPlayerLogEntry("news", "AI Error", "Failed to transmit: " .. tostring(err)) end) end
    end)
end

function menu.cleanup()
    menu.frame = nil
    menu.active = false
end

function menu.closeMenu()
    refreshHelper()
    if Helper and Helper.closeMenuAndReturn then Helper.closeMenuAndReturn(menu) end
    menu.cleanup()
end

-- NOTE: registration is DEFERRED (Helper is nil at file load). The companion poll tick + open path
-- call menu.ensureRegistered(). No file-scope registration.
return menu
